criteria_results.criterion: Migration 018 defines clone_workspace SECURITY DEFINER function
criteria_results.evidence: 018_clone_workspace.sql: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION clone_workspace(p_source_workspace_id UUID) ... $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER. NOTE: spec said (p_workspace_id, p_user_id, p_org_id) but actual signature is (p_source_workspace_id) with auth.uid() used internally — functionally correct and more secure, spec was aspirational.

criteria_results.criterion: clone_workspace copies 13 tables with UUID remapping
criteria_results.evidence: Migration 018 copies: workspace, tabs, sections, steps, connections, teams, roles, people, tools, step_roles, stages, touchpoints, touchpoint_connections (13 tables). 7 temp mapping tables with ON COMMIT DROP: _cwt_tab, _cwt_section, _cwt_step, _cwt_team, _cwt_role, _cwt_stage, _cwt_touchpoint.

criteria_results.criterion: clone_workspace does NOT copy: perspectives, annotations, comments, tasks, runbooks, activity_log
criteria_results.evidence: Migration 018 header comment explicitly lists excluded tables: perspectives, annotations, comments, tasks, runbooks, activity_log, public_shares. No INSERT into any of these tables in function body.

criteria_results.criterion: Clone API route exists with auth guard
criteria_results.evidence: src/app/api/v1/workspaces/[id]/clone/route.ts: POST handler, checks user via supabase.auth.getUser(), returns errorResponse('unauthorized', ..., 401) if no user.

criteria_results.criterion: Clone route calls supabase.rpc('clone_workspace') and returns via successResponse
criteria_results.evidence: route.ts line 18: supabase.rpc('clone_workspace', { p_source_workspace_id: id }). Fetches new workspace then returns successResponse(workspace) at line 54.

criteria_results.criterion: Clone route calls void logActivity() for audit trail
criteria_results.evidence: route.ts lines 43-52: void logActivity({ supabase, workspace_id: newWorkspaceId, user_id: user.id, action: 'created', entity_type: 'workspaces', ... details: { cloned_from: id } }).

criteria_results.criterion: Client wrapper cloneWorkspace(id) calls POST via apiFetch
criteria_results.evidence: src/lib/api/client.ts line 65-67: export async function cloneWorkspace(id: string): Promise<Workspace> { return apiFetch<Workspace>(`/api/v1/workspaces/${id}/clone`, { method: 'POST' }); }

criteria_results.criterion: Settings page: Duplicate Workspace button with loading state and navigation
criteria_results.evidence: settings/page.tsx: 'Duplicate Workspace' section exists (line 232), Button onClick={handleClone} loading={cloning}, cloning useState controls disabled state. handleClone: confirm() dialog, setCloning(true), cloneWorkspace(workspace.id), router.push('/w/${newWorkspace.id}'), try/catch with toastError.

criteria_results.criterion: Migration 019 creates coloring_rules table with criteria_type enum
criteria_results.evidence: 019_coloring_rules.sql: CREATE TYPE criteria_type AS ENUM ('status', 'executor', 'step_type', 'has_role', 'maturity_below', 'maturity_above'). CREATE TABLE coloring_rules with all required columns. RLS policies using can_access_workspace(). Index on workspace_id.

criteria_results.criterion: Coloring rules CRUD API: GET + POST (route.ts) and PATCH + DELETE ([id]/route.ts)
criteria_results.evidence: src/app/api/v1/coloring-rules/route.ts: GET (workspace_id query param, orders by position) + POST (full validation). [id]/route.ts: PATCH (EDITABLE_FIELDS whitelist) + DELETE (with logActivity).

criteria_results.criterion: HEX_COLOR_REGEX validation in POST and PATCH handlers
criteria_results.evidence: route.ts line 8: const HEX_COLOR_REGEX = /^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$/i; — validated in POST at line 60. [id]/route.ts line 6: same regex — validated in PATCH at line 35.

criteria_results.criterion: VALID_CRITERIA_TYPES validation in PATCH handler
criteria_results.evidence: [id]/route.ts lines 7-8: VALID_CRITERIA_TYPES const array; validated in PATCH at lines 39-47.

criteria_results.criterion: ColoringTintContext defined in canvas.ts
criteria_results.evidence: src/types/canvas.ts line 17: export const ColoringTintContext = createContext<Map<string, string>>(new Map()); with comment explaining the pattern.

criteria_results.criterion: canvas-view.tsx fetches rules, evaluates in position order (last match wins), provides tint map via context
criteria_results.evidence: canvas-view.tsx: fetchColoringRules(workspaceId) in useEffect. coloringTints useMemo iterates steps then activeRules (ordered by position from API). For each step, iterates all rules so last matching rule wins (map.set overwrites). ColoringTintContext.Provider wraps the tree.

criteria_results.criterion: step-node.tsx consumes ColoringTintContext, applies 15% opacity background (hex alpha 26)
criteria_results.evidence: step-node.tsx lines 35-53: coloringTints = useContext(ColoringTintContext); coloringTint = coloringTints.get(step.id) ?? null; style applies backgroundColor: `${coloringTint}26` (0x26 = 38/255 ≈ 15% opacity).

criteria_results.criterion: Heat map precedence: coloring tint suppressed when heat map active
criteria_results.evidence: step-node.tsx style ternary: heatMapMode && maturityColor checked first → maturity background; then coloringTint check; then default. Heat map fully overrides coloring tint.

criteria_results.criterion: coloring-panel.tsx accessible via paintbrush button (absolute overlay z-10), ~347 lines
criteria_results.evidence: coloring-panel.tsx: 347 lines. canvas-view.tsx line 249: <div className='absolute top-2.5 right-2.5 z-10 flex flex-col items-end gap-1'>. Button aria-label='Toggle coloring rules' with Paintbrush icon. ColoringPanel rendered beneath.

criteria_results.criterion: Process canvas only (canvas-view.tsx, not journey-canvas-view.tsx)
criteria_results.evidence: ColoringTintContext, fetchColoringRules, ColoringPanel imports only found in canvas-view.tsx (grep confirms 3 files: canvas.ts for type def, canvas-view.tsx for usage, step-node.tsx for consumption). journey-canvas-view.tsx not in grep results.

criteria_results.criterion: page.tsx select uses: '*, users!activity_log_user_id_fkey(email)'
criteria_results.evidence: activity/page.tsx line 25: .select('*, users!activity_log_user_id_fkey(email)') — exact match.

criteria_results.criterion: route.ts select uses identical shape: '*, users!activity_log_user_id_fkey(email)'
criteria_results.evidence: api/v1/activity/route.ts line 32: .select('*, users!activity_log_user_id_fkey(email)') — exact match, parity confirmed.

criteria_results.criterion: activity-view.tsx renders entry.users?.email (not 'Unknown' for valid entries)
criteria_results.evidence: activity-view.tsx line 176: {entry.users?.email ?? 'Unknown'}. Optional chain with nullish fallback — valid entries with FK join data show email; only genuinely missing joins show 'Unknown'.

criteria_results.criterion: Empty state guidance text matches spec
criteria_results.evidence: activity-view.tsx lines 147-149: exact text 'Actions like creating steps, adding comments, and completing runbooks appear here.' — only shown when activeFilter === 'all'.

criteria_results.criterion: Load More re-fetches from API with same joined select shape
criteria_results.evidence: activity-view.tsx loadMore(): calls fetchActivityLog(workspaceId, { limit: 50, offset: entries.length }) which hits the API route. Route uses the same joined select. Parity maintained.

criteria_results.criterion: Types: ColoringRule, CriteriaType, ActivityLog, ActivityAction, RunbookStatus, RunbookStepStatus exported from database.ts
criteria_results.evidence: database.ts: CriteriaType (line 329), ColoringRule interface (line 331), ActivityAction (line 310), ActivityLog interface (line 312), RunbookStatus (line 277), RunbookStepStatus (line 278) — all present.

criteria_results.criterion: WorkspaceProvider + useWorkspace exported from workspace-context.tsx
criteria_results.evidence: workspace-context.tsx: export function useWorkspace() (line 24), export function WorkspaceProvider (line 41).

criteria_results.criterion: Sidebar nav: all required items present
criteria_results.evidence: sidebar.tsx NAV_ITEMS array includes: Dashboard, Workflows (canvas), Compare, Comments, Runbooks, Activity, Teams, People, Tools, Settings — all required items present.

criteria_results.criterion: Workspace shell reserved paths array includes 'activity'
criteria_results.evidence: workspace-shell.tsx line 47: array includes 'teams', 'people', 'tools', 'settings', 'list', 'gap-analysis', 'compare', 'comments', 'dashboard', 'runbooks', 'activity' — 'activity' confirmed in list.

pages_checked: static analysis only — Playwright not required for this iteration

improvements_found.observation: The confirm() dialog text says 'A full copy will be created with all tabs, sections, steps, and connections.' but the clone also copies teams, roles, people, tools, stages, and touchpoints. The description understates what's cloned.
improvements_found.design_principle: Nielsen H1: Visibility of system status — user should know what is included in the clone.
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Update confirm text to: 'A full copy will be created with all canvas data — tabs, sections, steps, connections, teams, roles, people, and tools.'

improvements_found.observation: Load More fetches a fixed next page of 50 but the user cannot tell how many total entries exist, so there's no indication of when they'll reach the end beyond the button disappearing.
improvements_found.design_principle: Nielsen H1: Visibility of system status
improvements_found.suggested_approach: Show total count in header badge, or add subtle text like '50 of N' when total is known via a count query.

_notes.method: Static analysis — all suites used file-read approach per EXECUTION_PLAN method specification ('Static analysis of migration, API route, client wrapper, settings page'). Playwright not invoked.
_notes.spec_discrepancy: FEAT-050 acceptance check #1 says function signature is clone_workspace(p_workspace_id, p_user_id, p_org_id) but actual migration uses clone_workspace(p_source_workspace_id) with auth.uid() called internally. Implementation is correct and more secure — spec description was aspirational/outdated.
outstanding_warnings: Migrations 014-019 still need 'supabase db push' to remote DB before production use
Accessibility cadence severely overdue (last audit iter 21, now iter 97)
